cases.title: Participant reads own registration status for an event
preconditions: Published event in RegistrationOpen or later state
Participant A registered for the event
Valid Participant A JWT with sub matching registration participantId
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status with Participant A Authorization bearer
Inspect response registrationId and participantId fields
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with registration snapshot for Participant A only; participantId in response matches JWT sub; satisfies FR-26 own registration data access

cases.title: GET /me/registrations returns only the authenticated actor registrations
preconditions: Participant A registered for at least two events
Participant B registered for a different event not shared with A
Valid Participant A JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant A Authorization bearer
Verify every item registrationId belongs to Participant A
cases.expected: HTTP 200 paginated envelope; items contain only Participant A registrations across events; no Participant B rows; AC-16 view own registrations flow supported

cases.title: Event registration binds to JWT subject not caller-supplied participantId
preconditions: Published event with available capacity
Valid Participant A JWT
Optional participantId field in request body set to a different user UUID
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant A Authorization bearer and body containing foreign participantId if supported
Read returned registration participantId
cases.expected: HTTP 200; created registration participantId equals JWT sub (Participant A); foreign participantId ignored or rejected; participant operates only on own registration data per API register contract

cases.title: Participant successfully cancels own registration before deadline
preconditions: Participant A Registered for event before CancellationDeadline
Valid Participant A JWT
Fresh Idempotency-Key header
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{ownRegistrationId}/cancel with Participant A Authorization bearer
GET registration-status for Participant A
cases.expected: HTTP 200; registration transitions to CancelledByUser; only own registration affected; Cancel own registration capability per permission matrix

cases.title: Participant submits feedback on own attended registration
preconditions: Completed event with active feedback window
Participant A in Attended state with own registrationId
Valid Participant A JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/feedback with Participant A token and own registrationId in body
Verify feedback persisted for that registration
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with feedbackId and submittedAt; feedback linked to Participant A registration only; Submit feedback own registration only per permission matrix

cases.title: Participant self-check-in succeeds on own registration when enabled
preconditions: Event InProgress with self-check-in enabled and active check-in window
Participant A Registered with no prior check-in
Valid Participant A JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/self-checkin with Participant A Authorization bearer
GET registration-status for Participant A
cases.expected: HTTP 200; check-in recorded for Participant A registration only; registration moves to CheckedIn; self-check-in scoped to current actor per permission matrix

cases.title: GET eligibility/me returns only current actor eligibility
preconditions: Completed event with evaluated eligibility for multiple participants
Participant A authenticated with known eligibility outcome
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/eligibility/me with Participant A Authorization bearer
Compare result to organizer eligibility list entry for same participantId only
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with eligibility snapshot for Participant A; participantId matches JWT sub; no other participant eligibility data exposed

cases.title: Registration scope check runs before domain mutation on participant commands
preconditions: Protected participant command handler wired with authorization decision pattern (authenticate, role, scope, guard, execute)
Participant B JWT attempting mutation on Participant A registrationId
steps: Invoke cancel or feedback submit with Participant B token targeting A registrationId
Confirm handler short-circuits at registration scope step
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404 returned before state-transition validators or domain transaction; no registration mutation; satisfies NFR-08 authorization by role and event scope step 3

cases.title: Cross-participant registration mutation classified as P0 unauthorized critical update
preconditions: Two participants A and B registered for same event
Valid Participant B JWT
Participant A registration in Registered state
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationIdOfA}/cancel with Participant B Authorization bearer
Verify Participant A registration state unchanged
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404; Participant A registration remains Registered; classified P0 defect if cancel succeeded per testing plan defect severity model unauthorized critical update

cases.title: Out-of-scope registration denial uses typed error without leaking other participant data
preconditions: Participant B JWT
Known registrationId belonging to Participant A
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationIdOfA}/cancel with Participant B token
Parse JSON error body
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404 with error.code FORBIDDEN or not-found equivalent, user-safe message, and requestId; response does not include Participant A PII or registration details; immediate fail feedback per NFR-13

cases.title: assertRegistrationScope permits participant for own registrationId
preconditions: JwtPayload role Participant with sub equal to registration participantId
Valid registrationId UUID owned by that participant
steps: Call assertRegistrationScope(participantActor, ownRegistrationId, registrationParticipantId)
cases.expected: No exception thrown; participant may access own registration-related records per scope constraints section 3

cases.title: assertRegistrationScope rejects participant for another participant registrationId
preconditions: JwtPayload role Participant with sub for participant B
registrationParticipantId belonging to participant A where A sub differs from B
steps: Call assertRegistrationScope(participantBActor, registrationIdOfA, participantAId)
cases.expected: ApiError thrown with code FORBIDDEN and statusCode 403; participant cannot access another participant registration data

cases.title: Participant cannot cancel another participant registration
preconditions: Participant A Registered for event
Participant B authenticated with valid JWT
Participant B does not own registrationId of A
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationIdOfA}/cancel using Participant B Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404; Participant A registration state unchanged Registered; FR-26 own-data scope prevents cross-participant cancellation

cases.title: Participant cannot submit feedback for another participant registration
preconditions: Completed event with two Attended participants A and B
Active feedback window
Authenticated as participant A
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/feedback with Participant A token and registrationId belonging to participant B
cases.expected: Rejected with FEEDBACK_NOT_ALLOWED or HTTP 403; no feedback row created for B; scope constraint FR-26 enforced

cases.title: Participant cannot self-check-in using another participant registration context
preconditions: Event with self-check-in enabled and active window
Participant A Registered; Participant B also Registered
Participant B JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/self-checkin with Participant B token while attempting to target or infer Participant A registration if body allows registrationId
cases.expected: Only Participant B own registration checked in or request rejected if spoofing attempted; Participant A registration remains Registered without check-in; FR-26 own-data scope on check-in records

cases.title: Register request with mismatched participantId does not create registration for another user
preconditions: Published event with available capacity
Participant A JWT
Request body participantId set to Participant C UUID (not A)
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant A token and foreign participantId
Query registrations for Participant C
cases.expected: Registration created for Participant A only or request rejected with INVALID_INPUT; no registration row created under Participant C identity; participantId optional-if-derived-from-token per API contract

cases.title: Participant denied organizer operational registrations list for event
preconditions: Event with multiple participant registrations
Valid Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; no cross-participant operational registration data returned; participant scope limited to own records not organizer paginated list

cases.title: Participant denied organizer attendance and waitlist operational lists
preconditions: Event with waitlist entries and attendance records including other participants
Valid Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/waitlist with Participant token
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/attendance with Participant token
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; participant cannot browse other participants operational check-in or waitlist data

cases.title: Participant cannot retrieve another participant eligibility via scoped endpoint
preconditions: Completed event with eligibility evaluated for participants A and B
Authenticated as participant A
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/eligibility/me as participant A
Verify service enforces actor.sub and does not accept foreign participantId parameter
cases.expected: Returns only participant A eligibility; no cross-participant data exposure; actor scope enforced per permission matrix Participant can only access own eligibility records

cases.title: Participant my registrations UI shows only signed-in user registrations
preconditions: Web app running with authenticated Participant session
Seed data includes registrations for current user and at least one other participant
steps: Navigate to My Registrations page after login
Inspect listed event rows and registration states
cases.expected: UI lists only registrations belonging to signed-in participant; no other participant names or registration identifiers visible; AC-16 signup login view own registrations end-to-end satisfied

cases.title: Dev sub alias participant-1 submits feedback on own Attended registration with registrationId in body
preconditions: Completed event with active feedback window
Participant registered and Attended under resolveActorId(participant-1)
Dev JWT sub participant-1 with Participant role
Request body includes registrationId for that Attended row
steps: Invoke feedbackService.submit(eventId, participant-1, { registrationId, answers: { overallRating: 4 } }, participantContext)
Query persisted feedback row for the registration
cases.expected: Submission succeeds with feedbackId and submittedAt; not rejected with FORBIDDEN or message about own registration; ownership check uses actorIdsMatch (alias-aware), not strict string compare on participantId

cases.title: POST feedback with dev token sub alias and own registrationId returns HTTP 200
preconditions: Completed event with active feedback window
Attended registration for dev sub participant-1 (stored UUID participant_id)
Dev JWT with sub participant-1
Request body { registrationId: ownAttendedRegistrationId, answers: { overallRating: 5 } }
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/feedback with dev participant-1 Authorization bearer and own registrationId
Parse response status and body
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with feedbackId and submittedAt; response must not be HTTP 403 with ownership message when registrationId belongs to the authenticated participant; FR-26 own-registration scope satisfied for dev auth aliases

cases.title: Signup login view own registrations end-to-end enforces participant scope
preconditions: No existing account for test email
Published event with open registration
API and web app available
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register then POST /api/v1/auth/login for new participant
POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with issued JWT
GET /api/v1/me/registrations with same JWT
cases.expected: Registration created under authenticated user only; my registrations list contains exactly that event registration for the signed-in participant; AC-16 identity-to-own-data flow passes with FR-26 scope invariant
